WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Get distinct months per user
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS month_date
    FROM purchase
),
streaks AS (
    -- Identify consecutive month streaks using the classic gaps-and-islands technique
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) AS grp
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate length of each streak
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
user_max_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM user_max_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY longest_streak

    FROM race_counts
)

SELECT 
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.confrelid) AS base_table,
    base_cols.attname AS base_col,
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.conrelid) AS referencing_table,
    ref_cols.attname AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.confkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS base_cols(attnum, ord) ON true
JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.conkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS ref_cols(attnum, ord) ON base_cols.ord = ref_cols.ord
JOIN pg_attribute base_attr ON base_attr.attrelid = c.confrelid AND base_attr.attnum = base_cols.attnum
JOIN pg_attribute ref_attr ON ref_attr.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ref_attr.attnum = ref_cols.attnum
WHERE c.confrelid = (SELECT oid FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users')
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
  AND c.contype = 'f'

WITH draw_groups AS (
    SELECT
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (ORDER BY m.date, m.id) - 
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS draw_group
    FROM match AS m
),
with_goals AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        date,
        is_draw,
        draw_group,
        CASE WHEN is_draw = 0 THEN home_team_goal + away_team_goal ELSE 0 END AS goals_to_add
    FROM draw_groups
)
SELECT
    id,
    date,
    1 AS invoiced,
    SUM(goals_to_add) OVER (PARTITION BY draw_group ORDER BY date, id) AS amount
FROM with_goals
WHERE is_draw = 1
ORDER BY date, id

SELECT 
    t.team_long_name,
    COUNT(*) AS matches,
    SUM(CASE WHEN result = 'win' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS victories,
    SUM(CASE WHEN result = 'loss' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defeats,
    SUM(CASE WHEN result = 'draw' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS draws,
    SUM(CASE 
        WHEN result = 'win' THEN 3 
        WHEN result = 'draw' THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END) AS score
FROM team t
JOIN (
    -- Home team perspective
    SELECT 
        home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN 'win'
            WHEN home_team_goal < away_team_goal THEN 'loss'
            ELSE 'draw'
        END AS result
    FROM match
    UNION ALL
    -- Away team perspective
    SELECT 
        away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN away_team_goal > home_team_goal THEN 'win'
            WHEN away_team_goal < home_team_goal THEN 'loss'
            ELSE 'draw'
        END AS result
    FROM match
) m ON t.team_api_id = m.team_api_id
GROUP BY t.team_long_name
ORDER BY victories DESC

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Start from cards that have no previous card (no card points to them)
  -- These are our starting points
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    c.id::TEXT AS Path,
    c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM collection c2 WHERE c2.nextCardId = c.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursively follow nextCardId to build the path forward
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    p.Path || ' --> ' || c.id,
    p.start_id
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte p ON c.id = p.nextCardId
)
-- Only select paths that end at a card with no next card (complete paths)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL
ORDER BY start_id

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all ancestors of Superhero A (from A up to root)
ancestors_a(id, parent_id, depth) AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 
    FROM lineage 
    WHERE id = 1001
    UNION ALL
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, a.depth + 1
    FROM lineage l
    INNER JOIN ancestors_a a ON l.id = a.parent_id
    WHERE a.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Get all ancestors of Superhero B (from B up to root)
ancestors_b(id, parent_id, depth) AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 
    FROM lineage 
    WHERE id = 1201
    UNION ALL
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, b.depth + 1
    FROM lineage l
    INNER JOIN ancestors_b b ON l.id = b.parent_id
    WHERE b.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Store A's ancestors in a set for O(1) lookup
ancestor_set_a AS (
    SELECT id, depth FROM ancestors_a
)
-- Find the first common ancestor by checking B's ancestors in order
SELECT b.id AS common_ancestor_id
FROM ancestors_b b
WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ancestor_set_a a WHERE a.id = b.id)
ORDER BY b.depth
LIMIT 1

WITH RECURSIVE ManagerChain AS (
    -- Base case: start with each employee as their own starting point
    SELECT 
        id as employeeId,
        salary as employeeSalary,
        bossid,
        salary as bossSalary,
        0 as hierarchical_level
    FROM staff
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: climb up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        mc.employeeId,
        mc.employeeSalary,
        s.bossid,
        s.salary as bossSalary,
        mc.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM ManagerChain mc
    INNER JOIN staff s ON mc.bossid = s.id
    WHERE mc.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find managers who earn at least twice as much as the employee
    SELECT 
        employeeId,
        employeeSalary,
        hierarchical_level,
        bossid,
        bossSalary,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY employeeId 
            ORDER BY hierarchical_level
        ) as rn
    FROM ManagerChain
    WHERE hierarchical_level > 0  -- Only indirect managers (not the employee themselves)
      AND bossSalary >= 2 * employeeSalary
)
SELECT 
    employeeId,
    employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    bossid,
    bossSalary
FROM QualifiedManagers
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY employeeId

SELECT 
    begin_date, 
    end_date,
    GREATEST(0,
        (12 * (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM last_full_month) - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM first_full_month))) +
        (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM last_full_month) - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM first_full_month)) + 1
    )::integer AS full_calendar_months
FROM (
    SELECT 
        begin_date,
        end_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN begin_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date)::date 
            THEN DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date)::date
            ELSE (DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')::date
        END AS first_full_month,
        CASE 
            WHEN end_date = (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::date
            THEN DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date)::date
            ELSE (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) - INTERVAL '1 month')::date
        END AS last_full_month
    FROM race_dates
) t

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all items with their subgroups
item_subgroups AS (
    SELECT id as item, subgroup1, subgroup2 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL OR subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Build adjacency list (undirected edges)
edges AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT LEAST(a.item, b.item) as u, GREATEST(a.item, b.item) as v
    FROM item_subgroups a
    JOIN item_subgroups b ON (
        (a.subgroup1 IS NOT NULL AND a.subgroup1 = b.subgroup1) OR
        (a.subgroup2 IS NOT NULL AND a.subgroup2 = b.subgroup2)
    ) AND a.item != b.item
),
-- Recursive traversal to find all reachable nodes from each start node
-- Track path to avoid cycles
reach(start_node, current_node, path) AS (
    SELECT item, item, ARRAY[item]::bigint[] 
    FROM item_subgroups
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT r.start_node,
           CASE WHEN e.u = r.current_node THEN e.v ELSE e.u END,
           r.path || CASE WHEN e.u = r.current_node THEN e.v ELSE e.u END
    FROM reach r
    JOIN edges e ON r.current_node IN (e.u, e.v)
    WHERE CASE WHEN e.u = r.current_node THEN e.v ELSE e.u END <> ALL(r.path)
),
-- Minimum reachable node defines the group
group_assignment AS (
    SELECT start_node as item, MIN(current_node) as group_root
    FROM reach
    GROUP BY start_node
)
-- Final output with dense_rank for clean group numbering
SELECT 
    ga.item,
    isg.subgroup1,
    isg.subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ga.group_root) as group_id
FROM group_assignment ga
LEFT JOIN item_subgroups isg ON ga.item = isg.item
ORDER BY group_id, ga.item

WITH 
-- Rank all transactions by customer, gas station to find last per station
ranked_transactions AS (
  SELECT
    transactionid,
    date,
    time,
    customerid,
    gasstationid,
    amount,
    price,
    productid,
    cardid,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC
    ) as rn_last_per_station,
    -- Check if this customer has any issue
    MIN(CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN date || ' ' || time END) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid) as first_issue_datetime,
    -- Get gasstation of first issue
    MIN(CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN gasstationid END) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid) as first_issue_gasstation
  FROM transactions_1k
),
-- Get last transaction per gas station
last_per_station AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ranked_transactions
  WHERE rn_last_per_station = 1
),
-- For customers without issues, find their last transaction overall to get the gas station
customer_last_info AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    customerid,
    FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC
    ) as last_gasstation
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount >= 10
)
SELECT
  lps.customerid,
  lps.transactionid,
  lps.gasstationid,
  lps.amount
FROM last_per_station lps
LEFT JOIN customer_last_info cli ON lps.customerid = cli.customerid
WHERE
  -- Case 1: Has issue - show first issue gas station's last transaction
  (lps.first_issue_gasstation IS NOT NULL AND lps.gasstationid = lps.first_issue_gasstation)
  -- Case 2: No issue - show last gas station's last transaction  
  OR (lps.first_issue_gasstation IS NULL AND lps.gasstationid = cli.last_gasstation)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update the previous record's eff_to to be one day before the new record's date
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE - 1
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND eff_to IS NULL
      AND id != NEW.id;
    
    -- Set the new record's eff_to to the far future date (will be updated when next record is inserted)
    NEW.eff_to := TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    
    RETURN NEW;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER before_insert_team_attributes 
BEFORE INSERT ON team_attributes

EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH RECURSIVE bifurcated AS (
    -- Base case: all hero attributes with their chunk number and remaining value
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 50 
            ELSE attribute_value 
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN attribute_value - 50 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS remaining,
        CASE WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_bifurcated
    FROM hero_attribute
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: continue splitting remaining value
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN 50 
            ELSE remaining 
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN remaining - 50 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS remaining,
        1 AS is_bifurcated
    FROM bifurcated
    WHERE remaining > 0
)

FROM bifurcated
ORDER BY hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with the first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with next id
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount 
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END AS cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1 
            ELSE c.bucket 
        END AS bucket
    FROM cte c
    JOIN transfer t ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT id, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

              AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) 
                  AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1 - interval '1 month') 
                  AND date_part('day', timestamp) <= date_part('day', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 /
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1 - interval '1 month') 
                        AND date_part('day', timestamp) <= date_part('day', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0),
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior 
FROM transactions

WITH RECURSIVE ordered_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) as rn
    FROM sales_transactions
),
recursive_calc AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        rn,
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) as result
    FROM ordered_transactions
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: each subsequent row depends on previous result
    SELECT 
        ot.id,
        ot.trx,
        ot.event,
        ot.rn,
        ROUND(((rc.result + ot.trx) * ot.event)::numeric, 6) as result
    FROM ordered_transactions ot
    JOIN recursive_calc rc ON ot.rn = rc.rn + 1
)
SELECT id, result
FROM recursive_calc
ORDER BY id

WITH RECURSIVE 
nodes AS (
    SELECT id, parent_id, color, LENGTH(id) as depth
    FROM superhero2
),
-- Add row numbers by depth for processing order
ordered AS (
    SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY depth DESC, id) as rn
    FROM nodes
),
-- Iterative-like processing using recursive CTE with state
coloring AS (
    -- Initial state: just leaves with colors
    SELECT 
        o.id, 
        o.parent_id, 
        o.color as computed_color, 
        o.depth,
        o.rn,
        ARRAY[o.id] as processed
    FROM ordered o
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nodes c WHERE c.parent_id = o.id)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Add next parent that has all children processed
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        p.parent_id,
        CASE
            WHEN ch.cnt = 1 THEN ch.cols[1]
            WHEN ch.cnt = 2 AND ch.cols[1] = ch.cols[2] THEN ch.cols[1]
            ELSE 'gray'
        END,
        p.depth,
        p.rn,
        c.processed || p.id
    FROM coloring c
    JOIN ordered p ON p.rn = (SELECT MIN(rn) FROM ordered WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT UNNEST(c.processed)) AND parent_id IN (SELECT UNNEST(c.processed)))
    JOIN LATERAL (
        SELECT 
            COUNT(*) as cnt,
            ARRAY_AGG(c2.computed_color ORDER BY c2.id) as cols
        FROM coloring c2
        WHERE c2.id = ANY(ARRAY(SELECT id FROM nodes WHERE parent_id = p.id))
    ) ch ON TRUE
    WHERE p.id IS NOT NULL
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = c.computed_color
FROM coloring c
WHERE superhero2.id = c.id

WITH RECURSIVE nodes AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name AS name,
        parent_uuid,
        ARRAY[uuid] AS path,
        0 AS level
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case: traverse down the tree
    SELECT
        c.uuid,
        c.card_name AS name,
        c.parent_uuid,
        nodes.path || c.uuid,
        nodes.level + 1
    FROM card_type c
    JOIN nodes ON nodes.uuid = c.parent_uuid
),
-- Build the tree structure with children arrays
tree AS (
    SELECT 
        uuid,
        name,
        parent_uuid,
        level,
        path,
        -- Get direct children as array of records
        (
            SELECT ARRAY_AGG(
                jsonb_build_object(
                    'uuid', child.uuid,
                    'card_name', child.card_name
                ) ORDER BY child.card_name
            )
            FROM card_type child
            WHERE child.parent_uuid = nodes.uuid
        ) AS children
    FROM nodes
)
SELECT 
    uuid,
    name,
    level,
    path,
    COALESCE(children, ARRAY[]::jsonb[]) AS children
FROM tree
ORDER BY path

WITH RECURSIVE item_components AS (
    -- Base case: start with each item and its direct components
    SELECT 
        s.item_id as root_item_id,
        s.item_id,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        s.quantity,
        1 as depth
    FROM store s
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: expand uses_item_id to its components
    SELECT 
        ic.root_item_id,
        s.item_id,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_price,
        s.resource_price,
        ic.quantity * s.quantity as quantity,
        ic.depth + 1
    FROM item_components ic
    JOIN store s ON ic.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ic.depth < 10
),
-- Calculate costs: resources are always paid, items without recipes are bought at price
cost_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        root_item_id as item_id,
        SUM(COALESCE(resource_price, 0) * quantity) as resource_cost,
        SUM(CASE 
            WHEN uses_item_id IS NOT NULL 
                 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM store s WHERE s.item_id = ic.uses_item_id)
            THEN COALESCE(uses_item_price, 0) * quantity
            ELSE 0 
        END) as leaf_item_cost
    FROM item_components ic
    GROUP BY root_item_id
),
-- Partial craft: direct resources + direct item prices (no recursion)
partial_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0)) + 
        SUM(COALESCE(uses_item_price * quantity, 0)) as partial_craft_price
    FROM store
    GROUP BY item_id
)
SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    MAX(s.item_price) as retail_price,
    MAX(pc.partial_craft_price) as partial_craft_price,
    MAX(COALESCE(cc.resource_cost, 0) + COALESCE(cc.leaf_item_cost, 0)) as full_craft_price
FROM store s
LEFT JOIN partial_craft pc ON s.item_id = pc.item_id
LEFT JOIN cost_calculation cc ON s.item_id = cc.item_id
GROUP BY s.item_id

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::bigint::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN fe.end_date_key IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE to_date(fe.end_date_key::bigint::text, 'YYYYMMDD')
        END AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
month_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MIN(to_date(start_date_key::bigint::text, 'YYYYMMDD')) FROM fact_collection)),
        '2100-12-31'::date,
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    )::date AS month_start
)
SELECT 
    ms.month_start AS month,
    sa.set_name,
    COUNT(sa.set_name) AS existing_cards
FROM month_series ms
LEFT JOIN card_activity sa 
    ON sa.start_date < (ms.month_start + INTERVAL '1 month')::date
    AND (sa.end_date IS NULL OR sa.end_date >= ms.month_start)
GROUP BY ms.month_start, sa.set_name
ORDER BY ms.month_start, sa.set_name

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- All reference values from users
user_refs AS (
    SELECT id, ref1 as ref FROM users WHERE ref1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT id, ref2 as ref FROM users WHERE ref2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Build connections: users sharing any ref are connected
connections AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT u1.id as u1, u2.id as u2
    FROM user_refs u1
    JOIN user_refs u2 ON u1.ref = u2.ref AND u1.id < u2.id
),
-- Find connected components
component AS (
    SELECT u1 as root, u2 as node FROM connections
    UNION
    SELECT u2 as root, u1 as node FROM connections
    UNION
    SELECT id as root, id as node FROM users
),
-- Transitive closure
closure AS (
    SELECT root, node FROM component
    UNION
    SELECT c.root, conn.u2 as node
    FROM closure c
    JOIN connections conn ON c.node = conn.u1
    WHERE conn.u2 NOT IN (SELECT node FROM closure WHERE root = c.root)
    UNION
    SELECT c.root, conn.u1 as node
    FROM closure c
    JOIN connections conn ON c.node = conn.u2
    WHERE conn.u1 NOT IN (SELECT node FROM closure WHERE root = c.root)
),
-- Canonical component per user
user_comp AS (
    SELECT node as user_id, MIN(root) as comp_id
    FROM closure
    GROUP BY node
),
-- Merge by component
merged AS (
    SELECT 
        uc.comp_id,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.id) as ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) as usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) as refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref2) FILTER (WHERE u.ref2 IS NOT NULL) as refs2
    FROM user_comp uc
    JOIN users u ON uc.user_id = u.id
    GROUP BY uc.comp_id
),
-- All refs per component
comp_refs AS (
    SELECT comp_id, ids, usernames, refs1, refs2,
           (COALESCE(refs1, ARRAY[]::text[]) || COALESCE(refs2, ARRAY[]::text[])) as all_refs
    FROM merged
),
-- Asset counts
asset_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        cr.comp_id,
        cr.ids,
        cr.usernames,
        cr.refs1,
        cr.refs2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) as asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id) as asset_ids
    FROM comp_refs cr
    LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = ANY(cr.all_refs)
    GROUP BY cr.comp_id, cr.ids, cr.usernames, cr.refs1, cr.refs2
),
-- Orphan assets
orphans AS (
    SELECT 
        NULL::integer[] as ids,
        NULL::text[] as usernames,
        NULL::text[] as refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT owner) as refs2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT id) as asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT id) as asset_ids
    FROM assets
    WHERE owner NOT IN (SELECT ref FROM user_refs)
    GROUP BY owner
)
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids FROM asset_counts
UNION ALL
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids FROM orphans
ORDER BY COALESCE(ids, ARRAY[0::integer])::text
